hints: First, we name the direct object, then the adverb of degree (for example, very much).

a1.very much the food: ❌ The adverb of degree is not placed before the main direct object in such a sentence.
a1.the food very much: ✅ The direct object goes before the adverb of degree, so the order is natural.

hints: Place after the verb, then circumstance of time/manner (e.g., very late).

a1.home very late: ✅ First place (home), and then the circumstance of time/manner.
a1.very late home: ❌ The circumstance very late before the place sounds unnatural.

hints: Direct object (what?) before the adverb of manner (how?).

a1.very well English: ❌ The adverb of manner is not placed before the object in such a construction.
a1.English very well: ✅ The object (English) stands before the adverb of manner, which corresponds to the usual order.

hints: First the object, then the circumstance of place.

a1.in the corner that box: ❌ The circumstance of place should not precede the direct object in this sentence.
a1.that box in the corner: ✅ The subject is named first, and the specification of place is added after it.

hints: The circumstance of place usually comes before the duration of time.

a1.for a week in hospital: ❌ The duration of time placed before the place sounds unnatural.
a1.in hospital for a week: ✅ First we indicate the place, and then the duration of stay.

hints: The addition (what?) before the adverb of degree a lot.

a1.this music a lot: ✅ The object is placed before the short adverb of degree.
a1.a lot this music: ❌ Moving a lot forward violates the standard order.

hints: First a more specific place, then a broader context of time/event.

a1.at a friend's house after a party: ✅ A specific place before the circumstance of time/event sounds natural.
a1.after a party at a friend's house: ❌ Starting with after a party shifts the logic of describing the place.

hints: The addition before the adverb of manner.

a1.their victims very slowly: ✅ The object is placed before the description of the manner of action.
a1.very slowly their victims: ❌ Moving very slowly forward violates the typical order.

hints: First who/what was seen, then where.

a1.Hellen at the library: ✅ First we name the person, and the place is added afterwards.
a1.at the library Hellen: ❌ Starting with the place in such a simple sentence is unnatural.

hints: The addition before the adverb of speed/manner.

a1.very quickly the exam: ❌ The adverb is not placed before the direct object in this construction.
a1.the exam very quickly: ✅ The object comes first, and the description of speed is added afterwards.

hints: Order: object → place → frequency/time.

a1.my dog in the park every day: ✅ Direct object, then place, then frequency — a natural order.
a1.my dog every day in the park: ❌ Frequency before the place makes the sentence less natural.
a1.every day my dog in the park: ❌ Starting with frequency and immediately placing the object violates the typical order.

hints: First the object, then the direction/place, then time.

a1.her son to the doctor this morning: ✅ The child as the object, the destination place afterwards, time at the end.
a1.to the doctor her son this morning: ❌ The sentence starts with the circumstance of direction, making the order awkward.
a1.this morning her son to the doctor: ❌ The time before the object and place violates the standard order.

hints: Object → destination → time.

a1.home me after the concert: ❌ Starting with the place and then putting the object in such a sentence is unusual.
a1.me after the concert home: ❌ The time between the object and the place breaks the natural order.
a1.me home after the concert: ✅ Object first, then where, and only then time.

hints: The usual adverb of frequency is placed before the main verb.

a1.always finish school at 3.30 pm: ✅ The adverb of frequency stands before the verb, time at the end.
a1.finish always school at 3.30 pm: ❌ Splitting the verb and object with an adverb of frequency is undesirable.
a1.finish school at 3.30 pm always: ❌ Frequency at the end of such a sentence is rarely used.

hints: After the verb like, the object follows immediately, the intensifier at the end.

a1.don't play very often tennis: ❌ Another verb (play) does not fit the content about liking.
a1.don't like tennis very much: ✅ The object immediately after like, the adverb of degree at the end.
a1.don't like very much tennis: ❌ The adverb of degree is not placed before the object.

hints: The complement after the verb, the adverb of degree after the complement.

a1.very much loves his children: ❌ Starting with very much is unnecessary.
a1.loves his children very much: ✅ The object immediately after the verb, the intensifier of feeling at the end.
a1.loves very much his children: ❌ The adverb inside the phrase disrupts the sequence.

hints: Object → place → circumstance of time/event at the end.

a1.Italian in Rome during a student exchang: ✅ After the object comes the place, and then the description of the program/time.
a1.Italian during a student exchange progra: ❌ Time/event before clarifying the place makes the order less logical.
a1.in Rome Italian during a student exchang: ❌ Starting with the place before the object should not be done here.

hints: Order: verb → object → place → time.

a1.had a nice lunch at a restaurant today: ✅ After the verb comes the full object, then the place and time.
a1.had a nice lunch today at a restaurant: ❌ Time before the place at the end sounds less natural.
a1.had at a restaurant a nice lunch today: ❌ Inserting the place inside the object is inappropriate.

hints: Object after the verb, place next, time at the end.

a1.forgot the keys in the car last night: ✅ Keys as an object, place after, time at the end.
a1.forgot in the car the keys last night: ❌ Place before the object makes the sentence awkward.
a1.forgot the keys last night in the car: ❌ Time before specifying the place at the end is less logical.

hints: Adverb of frequency before the verb, object immediately after it.

a1.often takes a taxi to work: ✅ Frequency before the main verb, then the object and place.
a1.takes often a taxi to work: ❌ Inserting often after the verb and before the object is undesirable.
a1.takes a taxi often to work: ❌ Breaking the object with an adverb of frequency makes the phrase uncomfortable.

hints: After the modal part, we place the object, then the place, then the time.

a1.have to return the book to the library t: ✅ Object after return, place next, time at the end.
a1.have to return today the book to the lib: ❌ Time in the middle of the phrase makes it heavy.
a1.have to return to the library the book t: ❌ Place before the object complicates perception.

hints: Adverb of frequency after the auxiliary verb, place before the temporal circumstance.

a1.are never at home at lunchtime: ✅ Never after are, place next, time at the end.
a1.are never at lunchtime at home: ❌ Time before the place looks awkward.
a1.never are at home at lunchtime: ❌ Starting with never before the form be is not natural in a statement.

hints: After the verb be, we place the adverb of frequency, place, and time at the end.

a1.is always very hot here in July: ✅ Always after is, then the adjective, then place and time.
a1.is very hot always here in July: ❌ Adverb in the middle of the description makes the order uncomfortable.
a1.is always very hot in July here: ❌ Placing the place after the time sounds less natural.

hints: After the verb object, then place, and then time.

a1.play football in the park after school: ✅ Object football after the verb, place next, time at the end.
a1.play football after school in the park: ❌ Time before the place looks less natural.
a1.play in the park football after school: ❌ Place between the verb and the object creates an awkward order.

hints: Adverb of frequency before the main verb have.

a1.usually has cereal for breakfast: ✅ Usually before has, then the object and the circumstance of eating.
a1.has usually cereal for breakfast: ❌ Inserting usually after has disrupts the flow.
a1.has cereal usually for breakfast: ❌ An adverb in the middle of the object group sounds unnatural.

hints: After the verb the object, then the place, time at the end.

a1.met her husband at a nightclub in 2006: ✅ The object immediately after the verb, the place next, time at the end.
a1.met her husband in 2006 at a nightclub: ❌ Time before the place at the end is less natural.
a1.met at a nightclub her husband in 2006: ❌ The place before the object makes the sentence awkward.

hints: The frequency adverb before the verb work, the circumstances of place and time next.

a1.always work from home on Tuesdays: ✅ Frequency before the verb, then place and time.
a1.work from home always on Tuesdays: ❌ Placing always after the place makes the phrase less natural.
a1.work always from home on Tuesdays: ❌ Inserting always between the verb and the place disrupts the order.
